docs.title: Contact me
docs.text:
Want to work with me on a project?

Curious about my journey as a Technical Writer?

Feel free to reach out!

docs.title: Adaptiva documentation samples
docs.text: During my time at Adaptiva, I authored customer-facing documentation with a combination of GitHub and GitBook. All docs were written in Markdown files and include yaml frontmatter metadata to ensure optimized LLM ingestion and general SEO.

docs.title: Adaptiva Audience
docs.text: The primary audience for Adaptiva products are IT System Administrators and thus are written for a more technical audience. However, they still embody a beginner's mindset for a more junior or general audience as well.

docs.title: Links to Adaptiva docs
docs.text:
• New Feature How-To

    Demonstrates my ability to write concise how-to documentation with additional contextual information for a new feature.

    OneSite Patching Strategies

  • Network architecture conceptual piece

    On this page, I made a complex networking architecture concept approachable for a general audience, focusing on the 'why' behind each recommendation to encourage feature adoption and proper configuration.

    OneSite Platform Network Topology

docs.title: Meta documentation samples
docs.text: I developed internal hardware system documentation along with SOPs for research protocols while working for the Meta Reality Labs Audio team. Unfortunately, since these are internal and under NDA I cannot share those here, but I do have plans to make mock documentation to best represent what I learned and the work that I accomplished.

docs.title: OptiTrack
docs.text: OptiTrack is a motion capture hardware and software company. When I first started at OptiTrack I converted our outdated Wiki doc site to the more modern GitBook platform and over time curated and updated the documentation to more closely reflect the brand and the audience. This demonstrates my ability to independently lead a content management project to elevate an organizations user-facing documentation.

docs.title: OptiTrack Audience
docs.text: Since OptiTrack spans several industries (Entertainment, Robotics, Biomechanics, etc.), I made an effort to ensure anyone new to motion capture and our product had the opportunity to understand and build upon complex concepts. I am a firm believer in visuals from photos to diagrams in order to accomplish bridging the gap between experienced and beginner audiences.

docs.title: Link to OptiTrack docs
docs.text:
• Hardware system setup

    This hardware system setup page shows how I can document complex hardware setups with strict configuration requirements, using bold formatting and strong language to ensure user success. Additionally, it showcases my versatility as a technical writer to create system diagrams in line with company branding using Adobe Illustrator.

    Ethernet Camera System

docs.title: UW API Specialization Portfolio piece
docs.text: In the fall of 2025, I decided to expand the tools in my tool-belt and venture into API documentation. As part of my final portfolio, I was instructed to create documentation from a mock API provided by a classmate. From this, I created an entire doc site using MkDocs and Github and incorporated a AI chatbot that only ingests my documentation.

docs.title: Link to API docs
docs.text:
• OpenSpot API full docs site

    This doc site pushed me to learn how to develop an entire repository from start to finish utilizing GitHub automation and a static documentation site (MkDocs). Since this is a personal repo, it also gives me the ability to continuously integrate and develop to keep my skills fresh, or learn and implement something new.

    OpenSpot API Documentation
